llmTip: CRITICAL: When searching emails, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'from:', 'subject:', 'body:', 'to:', 'cc:', 'bcc:', 'attachment:', 'hasAttachments:', 'importance:', 'received:', 'sent:'. Examples: $search="from:john@example.com" | $search="subject:meeting AND hasAttachments:true" | $search="body:urgent AND received>=2024-01-01" | $search="from:john AND importance:high". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter IMPORTANT: Always use $select to limit returned fields and reduce response size. Recommended default: $select=id,subject,from,toRecipients,receivedDateTime,bodyPreview,isRead,hasAttachments. Use bodyPreview instead of body for listings. To read the full email body, use get-mail-message with the specific message id.
Creates a top-level mail folder. Use create-mail-child-folder to create a subfolder inside an existing folder. Use list-mail-folders to find existing folder IDs.
Creates a subfolder inside an existing mail folder. Use list-mail-folders or list-mail-child-folders to find the parent folder ID.
Renames a mail folder by updating its displayName. Use list-mail-folders to find the folder ID.
Deletes a mail folder and all its contents. This action is irreversible. Use list-mail-folders to find the folder ID.

CRITICAL: When searching emails, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'from:', 'subject:', 'body:', 'to:', 'cc:', 'bcc:', 'attachment:', 'hasAttachments:', 'importance:', 'received:', 'sent:'. Examples: $search="from:john@example.com" | $search="subject:meeting AND hasAttachments:true" | $search="body:urgent AND received>=2024-01-01" | $search="from:alice AND importance:high". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter IMPORTANT: Always use $select to limit returned fields and reduce response size. Recommended default: $select=id,subject,from,toRecipients,receivedDateTime,bodyPreview,isRead,hasAttachments. Use bodyPreview instead of body for listings. To read the full email body, use get-mail-message with the specific message id.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.

CRITICAL: When searching emails, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'from:', 'subject:', 'body:', 'to:', 'cc:', 'bcc:', 'attachment:', 'hasAttachments:', 'importance:', 'received:', 'sent:'. Examples: $search="from:john@example.com" | $search="subject:meeting AND hasAttachments:true" | $search="body:urgent AND received>=2024-01-01" | $search="from:alice AND importance:high". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter IMPORTANT: Always use $select to limit returned fields and reduce response size. Recommended default: $select=id,subject,from,toRecipients,receivedDateTime,bodyPreview,isRead,hasAttachments. Use bodyPreview instead of body for listings. To read the full email body, use get-shared-mailbox-message with the specific message id.

CRITICAL: When searching emails, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'from:', 'subject:', 'body:', 'to:', 'cc:', 'bcc:', 'attachment:', 'hasAttachments:', 'importance:', 'received:', 'sent:'. Examples: $search="from:john@example.com" | $search="subject:meeting AND hasAttachments:true" | $search="body:urgent AND received>=2024-01-01" | $search="from:alice AND importance:high". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter IMPORTANT: Always use $select to limit returned fields and reduce response size. Recommended default: $select=id,subject,from,toRecipients,receivedDateTime,bodyPreview,isRead,hasAttachments. Use bodyPreview instead of body for listings. To read the full email body, use get-shared-mailbox-message with the specific message id.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.

CRITICAL: This request requires the ConsistencyLevel header set to eventual. When searching users, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'displayName:'. Examples: $search="displayName:john" | $search="displayName:john" OR "displayName:jane". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes and set the ConsistencyLevel header to eventual! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter
Soft delete — moves to Deleted Items. To permanently delete, delete again from Deleted Items.
destinationId accepts folder ID or well-known name (inbox, drafts, sentitems, deleteditems, junkemail, archive).
Max 3MB. Body requires @odata.type: {"@odata.type": "#microsoft.graph.fileAttachment", "name": "file.pdf", "contentBytes": "<base64>"}.

Forward an email preserving full HTML formatting and attachments. The 'comment' field adds text above the forwarded content. toRecipients is required. Do NOT reconstruct the email manually - this endpoint handles everything server-side.
Reply to an email preserving full HTML formatting. The 'comment' field is your reply text. Do NOT reconstruct the email manually.
Reply-all preserving full HTML formatting. The 'comment' field is your reply text.
Create a forward draft (does not send). Useful when user wants to review before sending.
No request body needed — just call with the message ID. Draft must exist in Drafts folder.
List inbox rules (message rules) for a mail folder. To list rules for the Inbox, use mailFolder-id='inbox'. Rules automatically process incoming messages based on conditions and actions.

Create an inbox rule (message rule) for a mail folder. Use mailFolder-id='inbox' for the Inbox. The body should include displayName, sequence, conditions, and actions. Example conditions: fromAddresses, subjectContains, bodyContains, senderContains, headerContains, importance, isAutomaticForward, hasAttachments. Example actions: moveToFolder, copyToFolder, delete, forwardTo, markImportance, markAsRead, stopProcessingRules.
WARNING: Does NOT expand recurring events — only returns seriesMaster. Use get-calendar-view instead to see individual occurrences within a date range.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.

CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients. WARNING: Setting attendees replaces the entire attendee list — include all attendees, not just new ones.
Deleting a seriesMaster deletes ALL occurrences of the recurring event. To cancel a single occurrence, delete that specific instance ID from list-calendar-event-instances.
WARNING: Does NOT expand recurring events — only returns seriesMaster. Use get-specific-calendar-view instead.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.

CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients. WARNING: Setting attendees replaces the entire attendee list — include all attendees, not just new ones.
Deleting a seriesMaster deletes ALL occurrences. To cancel a single occurrence, use the specific instance ID.

Returns expanded recurring event instances (not just seriesMaster) within a date range for the default calendar. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use get-specific-calendar-view if you need a non-default calendar. To find Teams meetings, use $filter=isOnlineMeeting eq true. To search by subject, use $filter=contains(subject,'keyword'). Teams meetings include a joinWebUrl property needed for transcript access via list-online-meetings.

Returns expanded recurring event instances (not just seriesMaster) within a date range for a specific calendar. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Each instance includes seriesMasterId and type (occurrence/exception) fields for recurring event linkage. Use fetchAllPages=true to retrieve all results when there are many events. To find Teams meetings, use $filter=isOnlineMeeting eq true. Teams meetings include a joinWebUrl property needed for transcript access via list-online-meetings.

Expand a recurring event into individual instances within a date range. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use this to see all occurrences of a recurring event.
Returns a temporary download URL, NOT the file content directly.
Max 4MB. For new files use path format: /items/root:/path/to/file.txt:/content. Overwrites existing files without warning.
Body must be a full HTML document (with <html><head><title>...</title></head><body>...</body></html>). Partial HTML or plain text fails silently or creates malformed pages.
Body must be a full HTML document (with <html><head><title>...</title></head><body>...</body></html>). Partial HTML fails silently.

Lists all To Do task lists. Returns todoTaskList-id needed for all task operations. The default list is typically called 'Tasks'. NOTE: $select is NOT supported by this endpoint — do not pass select parameter, Graph returns 400.

Lists tasks in a To Do list. Requires todoTaskList-id — use list-todo-task-lists to find it. NOTE: $select is NOT supported — do not pass select, Graph returns 400. Use $filter=status eq 'notStarted' or $filter=status eq 'completed' to filter by status. Use $top to limit results. Status values: 'notStarted', 'inProgress', 'completed', 'waitingOnOthers', 'deferred'.

Returns a single To Do task. NOTE: $select is NOT supported — do not pass select parameter, Graph returns RequestBroker--ParseUri (400). Use $expand=linkedResources to include linked email/resource. Returns body content (HTML format), checklist items, and linked resources.
Priority values: 0=Urgent, 1=Important, 3=Medium, 5=Low, 9=unset.
Priority values: 0=Urgent, 1=Important, 3=Medium, 5=Low, 9=unset.
Response includes @odata.etag — save it, required as If-Match header for update-planner-task. Use includeHeaders=true to capture it.

CRITICAL: Requires If-Match header with the task's @odata.etag value, otherwise returns 412 Precondition Failed. Get the ETag from get-planner-task with includeHeaders=true. Priority values: 0=Urgent, 1=Important, 3=Medium, 5=Low, 9=unset.
Response includes @odata.etag — required for update-planner-task-details. Use includeHeaders=true.
CRITICAL: Requires If-Match header with ETag from get-planner-task-details (use includeHeaders=true). Checklist items use GUID keys: {"checklist": {"<guid>": {"title": "...", "isChecked": false}}}.
$filter only supports startswith() — contains() and eq on emailAddresses do not work. Use $search as alternative for broader matching.
emailAddresses array is replaced entirely — include all addresses, not just new ones.
Use contentType 'html' in the body — plain text contentType gets mangled by Graph API.
Use contentType 'html' in the body — plain text contentType gets mangled by Graph API.
Use contentType 'html' in the body — plain text contentType gets mangled by Graph API.
Use contentType 'html' in the body — plain text contentType gets mangled by Graph API.
Add $expand=fields to include actual column values. Without it, only metadata is returned.
Add $expand=fields to include actual column values. Without it, only metadata is returned.
List online meetings. Use $filter=joinWebUrl eq '{url}' to find a specific meeting by its Teams join link. Returns meeting IDs needed for transcript access.
Lists available transcripts for a meeting. Get the meeting ID first via list-online-meetings.
Returns the transcript content in WebVTT format with speaker identification and timestamps.

Lists recordings for a meeting. Each recording has createdDateTime, endDateTime, meetingOrganizer, contentCorrelationId (links to corresponding transcript). For recurring meetings, there is one recording per occurrence.
Returns a temporary download URL for the meeting recording in MP4 format. Use the download URL to access the video file. The recording may be large — do not attempt to stream it inline.

Lists attendance reports for a meeting. Each report has meetingStartDateTime, meetingEndDateTime, totalParticipantCount. For recurring meetings, there is one report per occurrence. Get the meeting ID first via list-online-meetings.
Gets a specific attendance report with totalParticipantCount, meetingStartDateTime, meetingEndDateTime.

Lists individual attendance records for a meeting report. Each record has: identity (displayName), emailAddress, role (Organizer/Presenter/Attendee), totalAttendanceInSeconds, and attendanceIntervals with joinDateTime/leaveDateTime. Use to determine who attended, how long, and when they joined/left.
Legacy — Microsoft recommends Teams channels instead of group conversations.
Legacy — Microsoft recommends Teams channels instead of group threads.
Legacy — Microsoft recommends Teams channels instead of group threads.
Copy a message to a folder. Body requires destinationId (folder ID or well-known name like inbox, drafts, sentitems, deleteditems, junkemail, archive).
Create a category with displayName and color. Color must be one of: preset0 through preset24, or 'none'.
Lists Focused Inbox overrides that always classify messages from specific senders as 'focused' or 'other'.
Create a Focused Inbox override. Body: {classifyAs: 'focused'|'other', senderEmailAddress: {address: 'email@example.com'}}.
Returns automaticRepliesSetting, language, timeZone, dateFormat, timeFormat, delegateMeetingMessageDeliveryOptions, and userPurpose.

Update mailbox settings like automatic replies (out-of-office), language, timezone, date/time format. For automatic replies: {automaticRepliesSetting: {status: 'scheduled', scheduledStartDateTime: {...}, scheduledEndDateTime: {...}, internalReplyMessage: '...', externalReplyMessage: '...'}}.
Get mail tips for recipients (auto-replies, mailbox full, etc.). Body: {EmailAddresses: ['user@example.com'], MailTipsOptions: 'automaticReplies,mailboxFullStatus,maxMessageSize'}.
Accept a meeting invitation. Body: {comment: 'optional message', sendResponse: true}.
Decline a meeting invitation. Body: {comment: 'optional message', sendResponse: true}.
Tentatively accept a meeting invitation. Body: {comment: 'optional message', sendResponse: true}.
Get free/busy schedule for users. Body: {schedules: ['user@example.com'], startTime: {dateTime: '...', timeZone: 'UTC'}, endTime: {dateTime: '...', timeZone: 'UTC'}, availabilityViewInterval: 30}.
Expand a recurring event from the default calendar into individual instances. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 format.
Create an online meeting. Body: {subject: '...', startDateTime: '...', endDateTime: '...'}. Returns joinWebUrl for participants.
Create a channel in a team. Body: {displayName: '...', description: '...', membershipType: 'standard'|'private'|'shared'}.
Update channel displayName or description.
Add a member to a channel. Body: {@odata.type: '#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember', roles: ['owner'|'member'], 'user@odata.bind': 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{user-id}'}.
Get the SharePoint folder (driveItem) that stores files for this channel. Returns drive-id and driveItem-id usable with OneDrive file endpoints.
Get the General channel of a team without needing to list all channels first.
Get current user's presence status (availability and activity). Returns availability: Available, AvailableIdle, Away, BeRightBack, Busy, BusyIdle, DoNotDisturb, Offline, PresenceUnknown.
Get presence for multiple users at once. Body: {ids: ['user-id-1', 'user-id-2']}. Max 650 IDs per request.
Archive a team. Optionally set shouldSetSpoSiteReadOnlyForMembers to make the SharePoint site read-only.

Create a new chat. Body: {chatType: 'oneOnOne'|'group', members: [{@odata.type: '#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember', roles: ['owner'], 'user@odata.bind': 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/{user-id}'}]}.
Get the current user's default OneDrive. Returns the drive-id needed for other file operations.
Create a folder. Body: {name: 'New Folder', folder: {}, '@microsoft.graph.conflictBehavior': 'rename'|'fail'|'replace'}.
Search for files and folders by name or content across the entire drive. The q parameter is the search query.
Copy a file or folder. Body: {parentReference: {driveId: '...', id: '...'}, name: 'new-name'}. Returns 202 Accepted with a Location header to monitor the async operation.
Update file/folder metadata. Use to rename: {name: 'new-name'} or move: {parentReference: {id: 'target-folder-id'}}.
Create a sharing link. Body: {type: 'view'|'edit'|'embed', scope: 'anonymous'|'organization'}.
Share a file/folder with specific people. Body: {recipients: [{email: 'user@example.com'}], roles: ['read'|'write'], requireSignIn: true, sendInvitation: true, message: 'optional message'}.
List columns (fields) defined on a SharePoint list. Useful to discover field names before creating or updating list items.
Create a list item. Body: {fields: {Title: '...', FieldName: 'value'}}. Use list-sharepoint-list-columns to discover available field names.
Create a new SharePoint list. Body: {displayName: '...', list: {template: 'genericList'}}.
Update specific fields on a list item without replacing the entire item. Body: {FieldName: 'new value'}. Use list-sharepoint-list-columns to discover field names.
Update user properties. To disable sign-in: {accountEnabled: false}. To update display name: {displayName: 'New Name'}. Requires User.ReadWrite.All scope and admin consent.
Revoke all sign-in sessions for a user. Forces re-authentication on all devices. No request body needed.

Add or remove licenses. Body: {addLicenses: [], removeLicenses: [{skuId: 'sku-guid'}]}. To remove all licenses, first GET /users/{id}?$select=assignedLicenses to get current SKU IDs, then pass them all in removeLicenses.
